title: abies brachyphylla var. umbellata
intro: Abies brachyphylla var. umbellata, also known as the mountain fir, is a subspecies of fir tree native to the island of New Guinea. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, derived from its essential oils and resins. The health benefits of Abies brachyphylla var. umbellata include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
